name: Barbell Back Squat
resistance_profile: Hardest near the bottom (deep knee/hip flexion), loading the quads at long length.
rom_notes: Squat to at least parallel; deeper increases quad and glute stretch if mobility allows.
cues: Sit between the hips, knees track over toes
Drive the floor away out of the hole
execution_steps: Set the bar across your upper traps in the rack, grip just outside your shoulders, and unrack by standing up and stepping back.
Stand feet shoulder-width with toes slightly out; take a big breath and brace your core hard.
Sit down and back, letting your knees track over your toes, until your thighs reach at least parallel.
Drive through your whole foot to stand back up, holding the brace until you lock out.
Re-breathe at the top and repeat, then rack the bar after your final rep.
good_when: You want one heavy driver for overall quad and lower-body mass.
You have a barbell and rack and want to progress with measurable load over time.
Building lower-body strength alongside size is a goal.
bad_when: You're a total novice who hasn't grooved the squat pattern yet (start with goblet squat or leg press).
Lower-back or mobility limits stop you from reaching a safe depth.
You're training alone to failure without a rack or safety pins.
common_errors: Losing the brace and rounding under load
